title: Ledermanniella keayi
what_description: Ledermanniella keayi is a plant species belonging to the family Podostemaceae. It is a semi-aquatic, terrestrial plant with aerial roots and submerged stems, found in tropical Africa. Botanically, it is characterized by its small leaves, 0.5-1.5 cm in length, with entire margins and a cuneate base, and its yellowish flowers, arranged in racemes, which are 1-2 cm long. The plant grows in waterfalls and rivers, and its stems and roots are adapted to absorb oxygen from the air. Ledermanniella keayi is a rare species, found in the tropical rainforests of Africa, particularly in Cameroon, Ivory Coast, and Ghana.
